mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment growth exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail auto sales combined with low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer credit expansion and demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate capacity utilization, which translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns during post-shock recovery phases or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >55% of high-inflation trigger months or the signal shows no predictive power when inflation exceeds the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of observations or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly correlates with aggregate corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain stress, which immediately depresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when industrial production drops below the threshold or shows positive predictive power in >50% of triggered cases.
